sn.s: Es git Wort, wo mer nöd eifach so seit.
sn.e: There are words you don't just say lightly.
"I love you" is one of them.

sn.s: Lang han ich's dänkt, aber nöd truue z'säge.
sn.e: For a long time I thought it, but didn't dare to say it.

sn.s: Hüt Aabig sitze mir am See, und d'Sunne gaht unter.
sn.e: This evening we sit by the lake, and the sun is setting.

sn.s: Lara leit ihre Chopf a mini Schultere.
sn.e: Lara lays her head on my shoulder.

sn.s: Es isch so still, mer ghört nu s'Wasser.
sn.e: It's so quiet, you only hear the water.

sn.s: Ich nimm all mis Muet zäme und säg's.
sn.e: I gather all my courage and say it.

sn.s: Sie luegt mich aa, und ihri Auge glänzed.
sn.e: She looks at me, and her eyes shine.

sn.s: "Ich liebe di au," seit sie ganz liis.
sn.e: "I love you too," she says very softly.

sn.s: In dem Momänt bin ich de glücklichschti Mönsch uf de Wält.
sn.e: In that moment I'm the happiest person in the world.

sn.s: Ich bi vo wiit här cho und ha öppis Schöns gfunde.
sn.e: I came from far away and found something beautiful.

sn.s: Nöd nu e Stadt, nöd nu e Sprach: au e Liebi.
sn.e: Not just a city, not just a language: a love too.

sn.s: Z'Luzärn isch jetz würkli mis Dehei.
sn.e: Lucerne is now truly my home.
